title: zigadenus elegans subsp. glaucus
intro: Zigadenus elegans subsp. glaucus, also known as the Blue Fescue Foothill Death Camas, is a plant species in the lily family native to the western United States and southwestern Canada. The plant contains toxic compounds, but its medicinal properties have been utilized to treat various health issues, including fever reduction and skin problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Zigadenus elegans subsp. glaucus include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antiseptic properties. Herbal preparations of the plant may include infusions and decoctions, although caution should be exercised when handling the plant due to its toxicity.
